summary: Full E2E testing of Bearer token auth fix (P0 bug). Tested: farmer registration → 4-step onboarding → dashboard, admin login → /admin dashboard, session persistence on refresh, logout clears token + redirects, protected page redirect when unauthenticated. All flows passed.
critical_code_review_comments: Console error: 401 on initial /api/auth/me call when no token is present (expected behavior but creates noise in logs - could add conditional check before calling /api/auth/me)
server.py still 860+ lines - consider splitting into route modules for maintainability
seed_data_creation: Created farmer farmer_1782766386@test.com with full onboarding data (farm, plot, device) via UI test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Bearer token auth fix (P0) verified working. Registration → /onboarding → 4 steps → /tableau-de-bord all working without redirect to /connexion. Token stored in localStorage as 'datagro_access_token'. Admin → /admin. Logout clears token via user menu dropdown (button text 'AAAdmin'). Protected pages redirect to /connexion when token missing. Backend 100% passing.
rca_of_the_issue: P0 bug was: cookie-based auth failed in Kubernetes proxy environment (httpOnly cookies not transmitted correctly). Fix: backend returns access_token in response body; frontend stores in localStorage and sends via Authorization: Bearer header. This bypasses cookie transmission issues entirely.
